metadata.description: Standards bypass configuration for prax branch

bypass.reason: Bootstrap/lifecycle logging - print() is used before the logging system is initialized and during shutdown. Logger cannot use itself for initialization messages (circular dependency).
This IS the display handler layer. unified_stream.py is the designated terminal output formatter for the monitoring system. console.print() is its core responsibility, not a violation.
Display handler for file change reporting. reporter.py formats and renders file change events to terminal using Rich markup. console.print() is its designated purpose.

Prax logging infrastructure — this file IS part of the logging system. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency). stdlib logging.getLogger() is the correct pattern for logging infrastructure internals.
Prax logging infrastructure — directly imported by logger.py (import chain). Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Prax logging infrastructure — directly imported by logger.py (import chain). Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).
Prax logging infrastructure — part of the terminal output subsystem within handlers/logging/. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency).

Prax import chain — directly imported by logger.py. Cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger (circular dependency). Logs route correctly via stdlib logger with prax naming.

Architectural — config/load.py provides shared constants (ECOSYSTEM_ROOT, paths) used by all prax handlers. It is shared infrastructure, not an encapsulation violation.
Architectural — imports ignore patterns config. config/ is shared infrastructure within prax.
Architectural — imports config.load (shared infrastructure) and trigger.modules.core (optional cross-branch integration with graceful fallback).
Architectural — imports config.load (shared infrastructure), trigger (optional integration), and uses module_name parameter for explicit caller attribution in monitoring context.
Architectural — module_name parameter used for explicit branch detection context. Auto-detection via inspect.stack() is not appropriate for a detection utility that receives context from callers.
Architectural — module_name parameter used for explicit module tracking. Auto-detection via inspect.stack() would defeat the purpose of a tracker that receives module names from external sources.
Architectural — integrates with watcher/monitor.py for file watching coordination. Both are part of the monitoring subsystem.
Architectural — optional trigger integration for file system events. Import has graceful fallback if trigger is unavailable.
Architectural — module_name parameter used for display attribution. Auto-detection via inspect.stack() would resolve to the display thread, not the originating module.
Architectural — imports PRAX_ROOT, ECOSYSTEM_ROOT from config.load (shared infrastructure).
Architectural — imports PRAX_ROOT from config.load (shared infrastructure).
Architectural — imports _find_repo_root from config.load (shared infrastructure).
Architectural — logging infrastructure imports config.load for path resolution. Part of prax logging internals.

Architectural — imports config.load (shared infrastructure) and uses module_name parameter for explicit direct-logging binding. Auto-detection is the default (get_direct_logger()), but the internal class accepts explicit names for infrastructure callers.
Architectural — imports PRAX_JSON_DIR from config.load (shared infrastructure).
Architectural — imports discovery/scanner for module discovery during logging lifecycle management. Uses module_name parameter for explicit lifecycle control of named modules.

Architectural — logging infrastructure imports config.load for path configuration. Uses module_name parameter for explicit logger setup per-module. Auto-detection is not appropriate for a setup function that configures loggers for specified modules.
Architectural — module_name parameter in get_module_logs_dir() receives explicit module names for log path resolution. This is the config API — callers pass module names, it resolves paths.
Architectural — imports _find_repo_root from config.load (shared infrastructure).
Architectural — imports DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL from config.load (shared infrastructure).
Architectural — imports PRAX_JSON_DIR from config.load (shared infrastructure). Uses module_name parameter for explicit filter context from callers.

json_handler.py IS the JSON template management system for prax. load_template() and json_templates/ are its core functionality, not a violation of the template-free standard. This file provides the template infrastructure that other files consume.

Monitor is a 3-thread architecture (display, file watcher, log watcher) that is tightly coupled by design. The threads share state (queues, positions dict, stop events) making splitting impractical without introducing complex inter-module synchronization. Direct JSON read of registry at line 344 is a one-time startup operation for branch discovery — routing through json_handler would add unnecessary dependency for a simple registry read.

notes.usage: Add entries to 'bypass' list to exclude specific violations

fields.standard: Standard name: cli, error_handling, log_visibility, handlers, etc. (required)
fields.lines: Optional - specific line numbers to bypass

architectural_notes.prax_import_chain: logger.py imports from: logging/setup.py, logging/introspection.py, logging/override.py, logging/direct.py, discovery/watcher.py, registry/load.py, config/load.py, json/json_handler.py. These files (and their transitive imports) cannot import from aipass.prax.apps.modules.logger without circular dependency.
architectural_notes.config_as_shared_infra: config/load.py provides shared constants (ECOSYSTEM_ROOT, PRAX_ROOT, path helpers) used by nearly all prax handlers. It is intentionally shared infrastructure, not an encapsulation boundary.
architectural_notes.trigger_integration: Several handlers optionally import trigger.modules.core for event firing. All have graceful ImportError fallbacks. This is cross-branch integration, not an architectural violation.
